title: Check if stdin is a term in --interactive --tty mode
body:
If you are attempting to run a container in interactive mode, and want
a --tty, then there must be a terminal in use.

Docker exits right away when a user specifies to use a --interactive and
--TTY but the stdin is not a tty.

Currently podman will pull the image and then fail much later.

Discussion in : https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8916

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Wow! (re: failing CI). 

There are scads of CI tests that unnecessarily add `-ti`. Those can be fixed - but the prevalence makes me suspect that this is a pattern that will be found in the wild, and hence this PR will break existing users. Proceed with caution.
We should make it a logrus.Errorf and not a breaking error for now. We can make it an actual error in Podman 4.0 sometime next year.
